2603.22868.title: Agent-Sentry: Bounding LLM Agents via Execution Provenance
2603.22868.paper_summary: Agentic computing systems, which autonomously spawn new functionalities based on natural language instructions, are becoming increasingly prevalent. While immensely capable, these systems raise serious security, privacy, and safety concerns. Fundamentally, the full set of functionalities offered by these systems, combined with their probabilistic execution flows, is not known beforehand. Given this lack of characterization, it is non-trivial to validate whether a system has successfully carried out the user's intended task or instead executed irrelevant actions, potentially as a consequence of compromise. In this paper, we propose Agent-Sentry, a framework that attempts to bound agentic systems to address this problem. Our key insight is that agentic systems are designed for specific use cases and therefore need not expose unbounded or unspecified functionalities. Once bounded, these systems become easier to scrutinize. Agent-Sentry operationalizes this insight by uncovering frequent functionalities offered by an agentic system, along with their execution traces, to construct behavioral bounds. It then learns a policy from these traces and blocks tool calls that deviate from learned behaviors or that misalign with user intent. Our evaluation shows that Agent-Sentry helps prevent over 90\% of attacks that attempt to trigger out-of-bounds executions, while preserving up to 98\% of system utility.
2603.22868.paper_summary_zh: 自主計算系統能夠根據自然語言指令自動生成新功能，正變得越來越普遍。雖然這些系統非常強大，但也引發了嚴重的安全、隱私和安全性問題。從根本上講，這些系統所提供的所有功能，加上它們的概率執行流程，事先並不為人所知。鑒於這種缺乏特徵描述，驗證系統是否成功執行了用戶的預期任務，或是執行了不相關的行動（這可能是由於系統被攻擊的結果）並非易事。在本文中，我們提出了Agent-Sentry，一個試圖限制自主系統以解決此問題的框架。我們的關鍵見解是，自主系統是為特定用例設計的，因此不必暴露無界限或未指定的功能。一旦被限制，這些系統便更易於檢查。Agent-Sentry 通過揭示自主系統所提供的頻繁功能及其執行痕跡來實現這一見解，以構建行為邊界。然後，它從這些痕跡中學習一個策略，並阻止偏離學習行為或與用戶意圖不一致的工具調用。我們的評估顯示，Agent-Sentry 有助於防止超過90\%的攻擊，這些攻擊試圖觸發越界執行，同時保留高達98\%的系統效用。
2603.22868.author: Rohan Sequeira et.al.
2603.22868.authors: Rohan Sequeira, Stavros Damianakis, Umar Iqbal, Konstantinos Psounis
